question: Which of the two coats is ________?
explanation: We use the "er" ending when we compare only two things.

question: Makala makes ________ stools at his workshop.
explanation: Adjectives follow this order: Opinion (beautiful) then Shape (rectangular) and then Material (wooden).

question: This is the ________ building in the entire town.
explanation: We use the "est" ending when comparing one thing to many others in a group.

question: The girls were afraid of the snake. (afraid)
explanation: This word is an adjective because it describes how the girls feel.

question: Nashawa bought a ________ walking stick for her grandfather.
explanation: The correct order is Size (long) then Colour (brown) and then Material (metal).

question: The room was big enough for the meeting. (big)
explanation: This word describes the size of the room so it is an adjective.

question: Of all the birds, the cheetah is the ________ runner.
explanation: Use the "est" form to show that one thing is the best out of a whole group.

question: Chikondi narrated to the children ________ stories.
explanation: The order is Opinion (exciting) then Origin (traditional) and then Purpose (moral).

question: The water in that stream is ________ and pure.
explanation: We use a simple adjective after the word "is" to describe the water.

question: He made himself a ________ sandwich for lunch.
explanation: We use the basic word "huge" to describe one thing without comparing it.

question: Those books on Asian art are mine. (Asian)
explanation: This is a proper adjective because it comes from the name of a place.

question: The questions were easier than we expected. (easier)
explanation: This word describes the difficulty of the questions so it is an adjective.

question: Which mountain is ________, Mount Everest or the Matterhorn?
explanation: We use the "er" ending because we are comparing exactly two mountains.

question: The hunter built himself a ________ mud house.
explanation: The correct order is Size (small) then Opinion (simple) and then Shape (round).

question: She received a written apology. (written)
explanation: In this sentence the word "written" describes the apology so it acts as an adjective.

question: The students appeared ________ before the start of the exams.
explanation: After a verb like "appeared" we use an adjective to describe the subject.

question: I have bought a ________ Dutch jersey.
explanation: An opinion word like "beautiful" always comes before a size or shape word like "tight".

question: The shop is now stocking ________ jewels.
explanation: The order is Opinion (precious) then Age (new) and then Material (golden).

question: This is the ________ book I have ever read.
explanation: "Worst" is the correct form when comparing one book to all other books.

question: The Hawaiian dancers wore colorful costumes. (Hawaiian)
explanation: It is a proper adjective because it is made from the name of a place.

question: The front tire looks ________ to me.
explanation: We use the simple adjective "flat" after the verb "looks" to describe the tire.

question: The ________ animal made the film exciting.
explanation: Adjectives follow this order: Size (enormous) then Opinion (scary) and then Type (land).

question: He bought his wife some ________ Bata sandals.
explanation: The order is Age (new) then Colour (green) and then Material (leather).

question: Track is my favorite sport. (favorite)
explanation: This word is an adjective because it describes which sport is liked best.

question: These apples are ________ than those ones.
explanation: Use the "er" ending to compare one group of apples to another group.

question: I found my friend reading a ________ story.
explanation: The order is Opinion (intriguing) then Age (new) and then Purpose (detective).

question: The ancient Egyptian exhibit was fascinating. (ancient)
explanation: "Ancient" is a descriptive adjective because it tells us about the age of the exhibit.

question: Which can run ________, the cheetah or the lion?
explanation: We use "more rapidly" because we are comparing how two different animals run.

question: He was a man of ________ ambition.
explanation: We use the adjective "great" to describe the noun "ambition".

question: She has married a ________ man.
explanation: The correct sequence is Opinion (handsome) then Age (youthful) and then Size (tall).
